channel.title: Authors on Justin Doty
channel.description: Recent content in Authors on Justin Doty

item.description:
I am currently a PhD Candidate in Economics at the University of Iowa. I will be on the job-market during the 2021-2022 academic year. My research interests include econometrics, applied econometrics, and empirical industrial organization. In particular, I am interested in semi-parametric and non-parametric models with endogeneity and unobserved heterogeneity. I am interested in applying these econometric techniques to the literature on firm productivity heterogeneity and auctions.

My job market paper applies non-parametric identification results to firm production functions when unobserved productivity cannot be separated from inputs. I develop a way to uncover the substantial variation across firms, corresponding to the rank in the distribution of output and input demand. My estimates capture heterogeneity in firm production and input adjustments with respect to productivity, which has not been explored in previous approaches.
